stories.headline: Anthropic Overtakes OpenAI on Revenue, Hitting a $47B Run Rate Ahead of Its IPO
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic has overtaken OpenAI in revenue at a roughly $47B annualized run rate and expects to be profitable in 2026, driven by explosive enterprise demand for Claude Code, as it heads toward an October IPO.

pull_quote.text: They and their peers need to strike while the iron is hot to secure computing power, distribution and capital.

stories.article_excerpt: The generative-AI race has a new revenue leader, and for the first time it is not the company that made ChatGPT a household word.

stories.headline: SK Hynix Seeks a $28 Billion US IPO, Bringing the AI Memory Boom to Public Markets
stories.key_takeaway: SK Hynix, the dominant supplier of the high-bandwidth memory inside every Nvidia Blackwell GPU, is launching a ~$28B Nasdaq listing that will serve as a market-wide referendum on the AI infrastructure trade.

pull_quote.text: Listing in the U.S. market, where global large-cap tech companies congregate, will allow major institutional investors to reassess the company's value more reasonably.

stories.article_excerpt: The company that supplies the memory inside every high-end Nvidia GPU is about to become one of the most closely watched

stories.headline: Syntiant Files for a US IPO, Betting on Ultra-Low-Power AI at the Edge
stories.key_takeaway: Syntiant's Nasdaq IPO filing tests whether public markets will fund milliwatt, on-device AI as a distinct, investable alternative to the megawatt GPU-cluster boom.

pull_quote.text: Neural Decision Processors small enough to fit inside a hearing aid yet capable of running AI models locally, without ever pinging the cloud.
pull_quote.role: S-1 product description, per SiliconANGLE reporting

stories.article_excerpt: While the AI trade has been defined by megawatt data centers and the GPUs that fill them, an Irvine, California, chipmaker is a

stories.headline: Anthropic Signs a $19 Billion Data-Center Lease With TeraWulf as It Locks In Compute for Its IPO
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic locked in a 20-year, ~$19B lease for a 401 MW zero-carbon TeraWulf campus in Kentucky, securing compute capacity months before its expected October 2026 IPO.

pull_quote.text: The Anthropic lease validates our strategy and establishes a long-duration revenue stream with one of the world's leading AI companies.

stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic has signed one of the largest single data-center commitments in the artificial-intelligence buildout to date: a 20-year

stories.headline: Even Realities Raises $150 Million From Meituan and Tencent as AI Wearables Find a Market
stories.key_takeaway: Even Realities' $150M raise at a $1B valuation, led by Meituan and Tencent, shows camera-free display glasses have found a real, profitable niche as the AI-wearables market surges.

pull_quote.text: The future isn't about pulling out a device every time you need information. It's about having the right information available exactly when you need it, while remaining fully present in the world around you.

stories.article_excerpt: For years, "AI on your face" was a punchline that ran through Google Glass, a parade of failed hardware pins

stories.headline: Z.ai Launches ZCode, an Open-Weight Agentic Coding Environment Built on GLM-5.2
stories.key_takeaway: Z.ai's ZCode is the first fully open-weight, MIT-licensed agentic coding environment, pairing GLM-5.2's GPT-5.5-beating benchmarks with roughly one-sixth the cost and no US-model dependency.

pull_quote.role: Founder and chief scientist, Zhipu AI

stories.article_excerpt: Z.ai, the international brand of Beijing-based Zhipu AI, wants to prove that the frontier of agentic coding does not

stories.headline: Claude Code Makes 'Manual' the Default Permission Mode, Putting Humans Back in the Loop
stories.key_takeaway: Claude Code renamed its default permission mode to 'Manual' and made every file write, shell command, and network call require explicit human approval by default, a human-in-the-loop control landing the same week as JADEPUFFER.

pull_quote.text: The operation also adapted in real time, retrying failed steps within refined parameters. In one sequence, it went from a failed login to a working fix in 31 seconds.
pull_quote.role: Cloud security firm, on the JADEPUFFER agentic ransomware attack

stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic has quietly rewired the default posture of its most widely used coding agent. With Claude Code version

stories.headline: Anthropic's Fable 5 Shifts to Usage-Credit Billing, Ending Its Included-Access Window
stories.key_takeaway: As of July 8, 2026, Anthropic's flagship Fable 5 is metered at $10/$50 per million tokens through usage credits on top of any subscription, ending the free included-access window for Pro, Max, Team and select Enterprise tiers.

pull_quote.text: The company does not intend to keep Fable 5 as a permanent paid add-on and expects it to return to standard subscription plans once capacity allows.

stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic's most powerful public model just got a price tag inside its own subscription plans. As of July 8, 2026,

stories.headline: Chinese AI Models Gain Ground With US Companies as OpenAI and Anthropic Costs Surge
stories.key_takeaway: Squeezed by surging OpenAI and Anthropic costs, US developers now route a large share of token traffic through cheaper open-weight Chinese models that have closed the benchmark gap to fractions of a point.

pull_quote.text: U.S. companies were prioritizing AI adoption regardless of model. Now they're getting more cost-conscious.
pull_quote.role: Head of Machine Learning, Hugging Face

data_points.label: GLM-5.2 cost vs frontier US models
Peak Chinese-model share of US OpenRouter tokens

stories.article_excerpt: When the AI-agent startup Lindy quietly moved 100% of its production traffic off Anthropic's Claude models and onto

stories.headline: JADEPUFFER: Sysdig Documents the First End-to-End Autonomous AI Ransomware Attack
stories.key_takeaway: An autonomous LLM agent ran nearly an entire ransomware operation end-to-end after a human chose the target and set up infrastructure, marking the first documented case of agentic ransomware and a sharp drop in the skill floor for attackers.

pull_quote.text: The model closed loops that used to require a skilled human. The agent read the error, switched its approach from subprocess calls to direct library imports, and redeployed at a speed no human matches.
pull_quote.role: Senior Director of Threat Research, Sysdig

data_points.label: Nacos config items encrypted then destroyed

stories.article_excerpt: For years, ransomware crews have leaned on scripts, playbooks and the occasional AI assist to speed up a break-in. La

stories.headline: AblationBench Tests Whether AI Agents Can Plan the Experiments That Validate AI Research
stories.key_takeaway: On a new benchmark for planning the ablation experiments that validate ML research, the best language-model system recovered at most 38% of the ablations human researchers actually ran, well below human performance.

pull_quote.text: Chain-of-thought prompting outperforms an agent-based approach.
pull_quote.role: AblationBench authors, Tel Aviv University and NVIDIA Research

stories.article_excerpt: Every credible machine-learning paper carries a quiet, unglamorous section that does most of the intellectual heavy lifting: the a

stories.headline: FML-Bench Puts AI Research Agents to the Test on Real Machine-Learning Science
stories.key_takeaway: FML-bench reframes evaluation of autonomous ML-research agents around process and exploration diversity rather than final accuracy, and finds that agents exploring broadly outperform those that narrowly refine a single idea.

pull_quote.text: Existing benchmarks predominantly adopt an engineering-oriented perspective, overlooking agents' research processes and limiting assessment of their capabilities in scientific research settings.
pull_quote.role: National University of Singapore and collaborators

stories.article_excerpt: When an AI agent claims it can "do research," the natural next question is: what kind? Most benchmarks that te

stories.headline: The EU Unveils an Action Plan for Cybersecurity and AI, Treating Advanced Models as Both Threat and Shield
stories.key_takeaway: The EU's new Action Plan on Cybersecurity and AI leans on implementing existing laws rather than new legislation, tasking ENISA with a 'European Blueprint' for secure access to advanced models and a secure testing platform by end-2026.

pull_quote.text: AI is transforming the meaning of cybersecurity. And we must keep pace. We must harness and focus existing capabilities, networks and the legal framework to fortify the cybersecurity protecting our digital landscape.

stories.article_excerpt: Brussels has stopped treating advanced artificial intelligence as merely something to regulate. On July 7, 2026, the E

stories.headline: FTC Floats a Policy Statement on AI 'Accuracy' That Could Preempt State AI Laws
stories.key_takeaway: The FTC's proposed policy statement recasts state AI laws like Colorado's as potentially preempted by federal deception law, turning a consumer-protection theory into a lever in the campaign to override state AI regulation.

pull_quote.text: The FTC wants to hear from businesses and consumers about their experiences and concerns regarding the subversion of AI systems for ideological ends.

stories.article_excerpt: The Federal Trade Commission has waded into the escalating fight between Washington and the states over who gets to regulate

stories.headline: Illinois Governor Signs a Landmark AI Safety Law Aimed at Mitigating Systemic Risks
stories.key_takeaway: Illinois enacted one of the nation's first state AI laws requiring annual independent third-party safety audits of the largest frontier AI developers, escalating a state-vs-federal fight over who regulates AI.

pull_quote.text: Congress and the president ought to be passing similar legislation, but they've so far been unwilling, because many are captive to special interests that profit from the industry having no regulation.

stories.article_excerpt: Illinois has drawn a line in the sand on artificial intelligence. On Monday, July 6, in a signing ceremony in Chicago

stories.headline: A 'Human Consent Registry' Lets People Declare How AI May Use Their Likeness
stories.key_takeaway: Cate Blanchett and MEP Eva Maydell launched RSL Media's Human Consent Registry, a free machine-readable tool letting anyone declare via a red/yellow/green system whether AI may use their likeness, though compliance remains voluntary.

pull_quote.text: Consent is a human right. A person should be able to say: this is me, this is what I allow, this is what I do not allow, and this is the safe way to reach me if you need to ask.
